relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: New tablets are carved like the first, after the shattered covenant.
Moses is called to the mountain in the morning.
The mountain is emptied for the encounter.
Moses rises with new stone in his hands.
YHWH descends in cloud and calls the Name.
YHWH proclaims YHWH: compassionate, gracious, slow to anger, abundant in loyal love and faithfulness.
Mercy carries sin, yet injustice is not treated as nothing.
Moses hurries to bow and worship.
He asks for Presence in the midst and for sin to be lifted.
YHWH cuts covenant again and promises wonder.
The people are told to keep the command as they enter contested promise.
They are warned against covenant with idolatrous systems.
Altars and standing stones of rival worship are to be torn down.
YHWH's jealous name refuses divided worship.
Idolatrous feasting becomes a snare.
Covenant intimacy can be pulled toward false gods.
No molten gods after the calf.
Unleavened Bread remembers the Aviv exit.
Every opener of womb belongs to YHWH.
Firstborn sons are redeemed, and no one appears empty.
Even in plowing and harvest, the seventh day stops work.
Weeks and Ingathering honor harvest seasons.
Three times yearly the males appear before Lord YHWH.
YHWH guards the land while the people appear before him.
Passover offering remains guarded from leaven and delay.
Firstfruits come to YHWH; life is not cooked in its mother's milk.
The covenant words are written according to YHWH's mouth.
Forty days and nights with YHWH end in the ten words.
Moses descends with tablets, unaware his face shines.
Aaron and Israel fear the radiance.
Moses calls them near and speaks.
The people receive what YHWH commanded.
Moses veils his shining face after speaking.
Before YHWH the veil is removed; before Israel, command is spoken.
The face shines after encounter, then the veil returns until the next speaking.

contemplative_note: Exodus 34 is covenant mercy after rupture: YHWH names himself as compassion and loyal love, Moses asks Presence to remain, and the renewed words leave his face shining.
